prompt: Implement issue #621: Krate agent identity web console pages, persona APIs, create wizard, and persona-aware existing UI. Depend on the #620 core identity contracts and preserve legacy AgentStack fallback.
issueTitle: Agent identity: web console - directory, profile pages, create wizard

existingUiReferences: DispatchButton shows persona names and avatars instead of only stack names
Run list shows persona display name, avatar, and role with stack fallback
Session detail header shows persona identity with stack fallback
NotificationBell uses persona display names in activity messages
Meeting participant list shows persona avatar, display name, role, and voice indicator when identity data is available

dependencyContext.issue620StateAtPlanning: closed after PR #661 merge according to issue comments
dependencyContext.issue621PriorPlanningContext: issue comments mention closed planning PR #666, open planning PR #723 on plan/issue-621, and implementation work in progress; this process should re-read the current branch before implementation and treat current code as source of truth
coreContractsObservedOnBranch: Krate SDK exports createAgentPersonaController, validateAgentPersona, validateAgentDefinition, resolveAgentPersona, and resolveAgentDefinition
Krate core tests reference AgentPersona, AgentSoul, AgentAppearance, AgentVoiceProfile, and AgentDefinition
Krate CLI MCP tests reference krate_create_agent and AgentDefinition/AgentPersona resources

acceptanceFocus: Directory page lists organization AgentPersona profiles with appearance/avatar data, role/tagline, definitions/deployments, run or meeting metrics when available, and a New Agent entry point.
Profile page resolves and displays persona, soul, personality, skills, definitions, appearance, voice, and recent activity without hiding missing optional resources.

Create wizard implements the documented seven steps and creates AgentPersona, AgentSoul, AgentAppearance, AgentVoiceProfile, and AgentDefinition with transaction or compensation behavior on partial failure.
Typed persona APIs use org-scoped authenticated route handlers, existing Krate resource/controller helpers, validation, cache invalidation, and resource events rather than UI-only storage.
Dispatch, run list, session detail, notifications, command palette, and meeting participants prefer persona display identity and preserve legacy AgentStack fallback.
All new components are exported through the component barrel, follow existing component conventions, and include expected accessible controls and states.
Tests cover route/page/component structure, API contracts, identity resolution, wizard partial-failure handling, and legacy fallback behavior.
nonGoals: Do not implement or modify the #620 core CRD/controller contracts beyond small compatibility fixes required to consume already-landed identity resources.
Do not remove or break legacy AgentStack dispatch, stack pages, inline prompts, or stack-based UI fallback.
Do not require external avatar generation or TTS providers for base workflow success; keep provider integrations behind explicit route contracts and mocks when needed.
Do not redesign unrelated Krate navigation, settings, meetings, inference, MCP, or agent stack builder surfaces.
Do not generate shell-oriented process subtasks for this repository workflow.

plannedPhases.name: Authoritative context
plannedPhases.purpose: Read #621, all comments and labels, dependency #620, and the agent identity docs before implementation.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Scan current Krate identity, web, API, route, component, navigation, MCP, and test infrastructure before proposing new surfaces.

plannedPhases.name: Runtime trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Map loadKrateUi, org navigation, API auth/resource routes, identity contracts, existing stack-centric UI references, and test conventions.

plannedPhases.name: Dependency readiness
plannedPhases.purpose: Gate implementation on landed #620 resource/controller/SDK contracts and raise a maintainer breakpoint only for real contract ambiguity.

plannedPhases.name: Acceptance tests
plannedPhases.purpose: Write failing or pending tests for pages, components, APIs, wizard rollback, persona identity resolution, and legacy fallback before implementation.

plannedPhases.name: Implementation slices
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement identity helpers, typed APIs, directory/profile/wizard pages and components, navigation exports, and persona-aware existing UI references.

plannedPhases.name: Quality gates
plannedPhases.purpose: Run web, Krate, CLI, build, metadata, and optional Playwright gates; iterate up to three attempts on failures.

plannedPhases.name: Review
plannedPhases.purpose: Review UX, API/auth, resource contracts, wizard partial-state behavior, provider stubs, accessibility, and fallback consistency.

plannedPhases.name: Final acceptance
plannedPhases.purpose: Compare the issue and specs to the final diff and verification results before accepting the implementation.

breakpoints.when: Only if #620 contracts are absent or materially conflict with the web spec in a way that would require inventing storage or changing resource semantics.
options: Pause until core identity contracts are clarified
